title: Nelumbo nelumbo
taxonomy: Nelumbo nelumbo is classified as a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Nymphaeaceae. This aquatic plant is also commonly known as the American lotus or Egyptian lotus. Nelumbo nelumbo is native to the wetlands of North America and is often found in shallow lakes, ponds, and rivers. The species is a perennial plant that thrives in full sun to partial shade and requires warm, humid conditions to grow. Nelumbo nelumbo is a popular ornamental plant, prized for its beautiful flowers and attractive foliage.
uses: Nelumbo nelumbo, also known as the sacred lotus, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The plant's rhizomes are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. In addition, the plant's flowers and leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may have antimicrobial and antiviral effects. The sacred lotus has also been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its purported ability to regulate gut health. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of the sacred lotus for medicinal use.

side_effects: Nelumbo nelumbo, the consumption of its parts, especially the rhizomes and seeds, can cause gastrointestinal problems and allergic reactions in some individuals. intro: Nelumbo nelumbo, also known as the sacred lotus, is a water plant native to Asia and Australia, characterized by its distinctive flowers and edible seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to lower blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and aid in the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Nelumbo nelumbo include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, with the rhizomes and seeds being the most commonly used parts for herbal preparations.
